latest.title: Trading Statement
latest.content:
18 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0438X
Dianomi PLC
18 March 2026
18 March 2026
Dianomi plc
("Dianomi", the "Company" or the "Group")
Trading and Publisher Expansion Update
Revenues In-Line and Improved Gross Margin Performance
Return to growth and profitability in H2 25
Dianomi, a leading provider of digital advertising services to premium clients in the Business, Finance and Lifestyle sectors, is pleased to provide an update on trading for the 12 months to 31 December 2025 ("FY25").
The Group expects to deliver revenues of £27.4 million (FY24: £28.0 million), in line with market expectations
*
. Strongly improved gross margin performance led to a year on year increase in gross profit to £7.5 million (FY24: £7.3 million), and an improved EBITDA loss of approximately £0.3 million (FY24: £0.3 million), substantially ahead of market forecasts*. Cash at year end was £5.8 million (FY24: £8.8 million) reflecting a return to profitability in the second half of the year. All FY25 figures are subject to audit.
While trading was held back by a subdued market reflecting broader macroeconomic conditions, Dianomi improved its margin performance through ongoing operational optimisations. The Group also expanded its product offering and continues to offer a very compelling proposition, underpinned by excellent distribution capability, reaching 500 million digital devices each month across a network of more than 250 premium publishers.
Looking forward, following successful trials and subsequent onboarding in FY24, post year end Dianomi has expanded its partnerships with both CNN News and Associated Press, adding additional units on new pages to its existing footprint across both publishers' wider sites, highlighting the attractiveness of Dianomi's solution to its publishing partners. The benefits from the expansion with both CNN News and Associated Press are expected to flow from Q2 FY26 onwards.
Furthermore, reflecting the focus on new product development and AI, a new partnership with AI media infrastructure company Dappier, was announced earlier this week, to launch an advertising monetised AI-powered financial answers engine designed for premium publisher websites.
By combining Dappier's conversational AI and monetization technology with Dianomi's scaled distribution across premium finance publishers and its financial advertising marketplace, the aim is to build a distributed AI answers network across the sector.
The Company looks forward to announcing its FY25 results in May 2026.
\* The Company believes market expectations for FY 2025 to be for revenues of £27.5 million, gross profit of £7.0 million and EBITDA loss of £1.1 million
Rupert Hodson, CEO of Dianomi, said:
"2025 saw a subdued market in which we traded well in the second half of the year following a challenging first half, returning to both growth and profitability. During the year we focused on investing in and developing our capabilities and our products. I believe the growth in our partnerships with CNN and Associated Press clearly demonstrate the value of the Dianomi proposition at the premium end of the market and the trust our partners place in our platform. Our focus is to fully leverage this opportunity. 2026 has seen some positive developments and we look forward to building on this momentum as the year progresses."
For further information contact:
Dianomi
Tel: +44 (0)207 802 5530
Rupert Hodson (Chief Executive Officer)
Charlotte Stranner (Chief Financial Officer)
Panmure Liberum (NOMAD and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)207 886 2500
Emma Earl, Corporate Finance
Rupert Dearden, Corporate Broking
Novella Communications
Tel: +44 (0)203 151 7008
Tim Robertson / Aeliya Birgrami
dianomiplc@novella-comms.com
About Dianomi
Dianomi, established in 2003, is a leading provider of digital advertising services to premium clients in the Business, Finance and Lifestyle sectors. The Group operates from its offices in London, New York and Sydney. The Group enables premium brands to deliver advertisements to a targeted audience on the desktop and mobile websites, mobile and tablet applications of premium publishers. It provides premium advertisers, including blue chip names such as Charles Schwab, Invesco and Bank of America, with access to an international audience of over 500 million devices per month through its partnerships with over 250 premium publishers, including blue chip names such as Reuters, CNN Business, the Times and WSJ. Adverts served are contextually relevant to the content of the webpages on which they appear and mirror the style of the page, which enhances reader engagement.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: DNM is a tech‑centric platform that delivers AI‑powered analytics for supply‑chain and logistics operations. Its core product is a cloud‑based dashboard that aggregates real‑time data, applies predictive models, and offers automated decision‑support to mid‑market manufacturers and distributors.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global supply‑chain analytics market is growing at double‑digit rates, driven by the need for visibility and resilience after recent disruptions. DNM’s solution plugs a critical gap by turning raw sensor data into actionable insights, giving its clients a competitive edge in cost control and delivery reliability.
company_30s.current_state: DNM has moved from a prototype stage to a revenue‑generating product, but it still operates at a tight cash burn. The company has recently re‑located its headquarters to a larger office space and is actively courting institutional investors to fund a next‑phase rollout.

rns_narrative: The March 2026 announcement of a partnership with AI specialist Dappier signals a strategic shift: DNM is integrating generative‑AI capabilities into its platform, potentially opening new revenue streams and raising its product value proposition.

Earlier this year, the company released a series of financial updates—half‑year results, AGM outcomes, and a trading statement—that all point to a steady, if modest, improvement in cash flow and a clear plan to scale operations.
The change of registered office in June 2026 and the disclosure of new holdings suggest a formal restructuring aimed at attracting larger investors and improving governance.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quietly hopeful – the community is largely silent but a few bullish voices hint at a future rebound.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders point to the AI partnership as a game‑changer and view the recent financials as a sign of stabilisation. They expect the platform to hit new adoption milestones in the next 6–12 months.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that DNM’s revenue remains too thin and that the partnership may not translate into immediate cash flow. Concerns about the company’s high burn rate and the lack of a clear exit strategy keep some investors wary.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the past year the chatter has moved from vague optimism to cautious observation. No new bullish catalysts have yet materialised, leaving the mood largely neutral.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – no recent spikes in search volume, which is a classic contrarian signal that the market is still unaware of any upcoming catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: A formal rollout of the Dappier‑powered feature set in Q3 2026 could spark a sharp uptick if it demonstrates clear cost‑savings for early adopters.
Positive commentary from a prominent analyst or a case study published by a large client would act as a credible catalyst.
An increase in trading volume that breaks the 30‑day moving‑average support level could confirm that the market has finally recognised the company’s value.
expect_next.watch_for: Keep an eye on the next quarterly earnings release for any sign of revenue acceleration; watch for a spike in shares traded that exceeds 200% of the 30‑day average; monitor the company’s social media for any mention of new big‑customer deals.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a post‑crash recovery: the price is still far below its recent peak and the volume base is thin. The APEX score suggests a tight compression, meaning a breakout is possible once the next catalyst hits.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: DNM sits on a deep crash that is almost 80% below its last high, creating a large upside potential if the AI partnership delivers. Historically, deep‑crash signals have produced average returns above 30% when the bounce occurs, and the company’s recent restructuring signals that the market may finally recognise its value. A rebound to the midpoint between the current low and the last high would deliver roughly 100% upside.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the partnership fails to generate revenue quickly, leaving the company’s burn rate unsustainable. If the stock fails to break the key support level within the next 90 days, the price could stay trapped at the current trough, and the company might need to raise additional capital at a lower valuation.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is roughly double the downside: a 50% drop from the current price is unlikely because the market has already priced in the crash, whereas a 100% gain is attainable if the platform scales as projected.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback that touches the 30‑day support while keeping a stop 10% below that level. Position sizing should be conservative – no more than 5% of the portfolio – given the high volatility typical of deep‑crash setups.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a clear volume surge that exceeds the 30‑day average and the AI partnership is officially rolled out to a major client. The trigger price would be the current level plus a 5% buffer to avoid buying in the tail of a false breakout.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a material downgrade, fails to meet a critical revenue target, or is forced to sell off key assets, consider exiting the position to protect the limited downside.

ai_insights.bottom_line: DNM is a deep‑crashed AI‑analytics player with a promising partnership that could unlock significant upside, but only if the market recognises the value within the next few months.
